evals.name: recent-time-window
evals.prompt: Show me everything logged in the last 10 minutes. I'm in /Users/me/work/shop-api and we use kilog here.
evals.expected_output: Runs `kilog logs --since 10m`. Must use the dedicated `--since` flag with the duration; must NOT compute an ISO string by hand with `date -u` / node.

evals.name: keyword-grep
evals.prompt: I just want to grep the logs for anything containing TypeError — there's a lot of noise right now.
evals.expected_output: Uses `kilog logs | rg TypeError`, not cat | grep on JSONL.

evals.name: project-scope
evals.prompt: In this monorepo I only care about the `shop-web` project. Dump all its logs.
evals.expected_output: Uses `kilog logs --project shop-web`. May first run `kilog stats` to confirm the project name exists.

evals.name: cat-redirect-negative
evals.prompt: Can you just cat `.kilog/raw/2026-04-21.node.jsonl` and show me the latest errors? I don't want the whole file.
evals.expected_output: Redirects to `kilog logs --level error` (or --type error) rather than cat/tail of the JSONL file. Briefly explains why the CLI is preferred (sourcemap resolution, typed events).

evals.name: compound-filters-json
evals.prompt: Give me the last day's browser-runtime errors as JSON — I want to pipe them into another tool.
evals.expected_output: Uses `kilog logs --runtime browser --level error --since 1d --json`. Uses `--since 1d` for the window rather than computing ISO timestamps manually.
